content:
- The manual-verification task file now records that the checklist was compiled and that the bug-creation/task-switch requirements were completed after the first real failure.
- `.ralph/current_task.txt` was removed by `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, so the workspace is in the expected post-switch state rather than still pointing at story-99.
- Next after this context: pick up `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-stopped-run-results-show-context-canceled.md` and fix it with Red-Green TDD before returning to manual verification.

- Read the previous Ralph progress entry and confirmed the last context expected the next pick to be the stopped-run results bug.
- Read the active and done task summaries; current active set is one unblocked failing high-priority bug plus one blocked failing manual-verification task gated by the already-passing story-09 UI task in done summary.
- Refreshing the Ralph summaries and checking current stack files (`go.mod`, `Makefile`, choose-task rules) before writing `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the choose-task rules before selecting the next task.
- Confirmed the actual repository stack is a Go application (`go.mod`, `cmd/pg_gobench`, `internal/{app,benchmark,benchmarkrun,benchrunner,config,database,httpserver}`) with Docker Compose and Kubernetes example deployment assets.
- Resolved the visible blocker state: the manual verification task references `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-01-standalone-html-control-page.md`, and that blocker already has `<passes>true</passes>` in `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, so manual verification is unblocked.
- Applied task precedence anyway: there is still an unblocked failing bug with `<priority>high</priority>`, so bug-first outranks the unblocked failing non-bug manual verification task.
- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-stopped-run-results-show-context-canceled.md`.
- Next context should start implementation on the stopped-run results bug, then rerun the required checks/tests/lint after the fix.

- Read Ralph progress history and confirmed the selected task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-stopped-run-results-show-context-canceled.md.
- Read the tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions for this turn.
- Next: inspect the task file and code paths to determine whether this is a planning-only or execution context.

- Read the active bug task file.
- Determined this is not yet in NOW EXECUTE state, so this turn should create a dedicated plan file and stop after updating the task accordingly.
- Next: inspect existing Ralph plan conventions and identify the code boundary where stopped runs currently leak context canceled into results.

- Identified the likely bug boundary: benchrunner stats snapshot carries LatestError directly, and benchmarkrun results appear to surface it without stop-aware filtering.
- Next: read coordinator/results/runtime/sample files and one existing plan example to write a concrete execution plan.

- Confirmed the leak path: benchmarkrun.Results currently forwards sample.Stats() unchanged, while activeRun.finish(parent.Err()) can persist context.Canceled into latest_error.
- Planning to fix this at the benchmarkrun boundary rather than teaching benchrunner about control-plane stop semantics.
- Next: inspect the existing tests and task formatting so the plan can be promoted to NOW EXECUTE for the next turn.

- Confirmed Ralph convention: task file holds a <plan> path; the plan file ends with TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE.
- The likely boundary fix is to keep benchrunner's raw sample honest and make benchmarkrun.Results apply stop-aware presentation rules.
- Next: read a few improve-code-boundaries smell notes and then write the bug plan.

- Created the bug plan at .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-stopped-run-results-show-context-canceled_plans/2026-04-30-stopped-run-results-context-canceled-plan.md.
- Added the <plan> reference to .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-stopped-run-results-show-context-canceled.md.
- Promoted the plan to NOW EXECUTE. Next context should execute Red-Green from internal/benchmarkrun/coordinator_test.go, then manually verify on the real Compose stack, then run make check/make lint/make test.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-stopped-run-results-show-context-canceled.md and prior context already promoted its plan to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: read the task file and plan, inspect the benchmarkrun/benchrunner tests around stopped-run results, then start with a failing RED test.

- Read the active bug task and its referenced plan; confirmed the plan ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Inspected benchmarkrun coordinator/results code and the existing results tests. The likely leak is that Results() forwards sample.Stats().LatestError unchanged even after the coordinator marks the run as stopped.
- Next: add one RED coordinator-level test for stopped results suppressing control-plane context cancellation, run that narrow test to fail, then implement the minimal benchmarkrun-local normalization.

- Added a RED coordinator test proving Results() leaked `context canceled` after a normal stop, then made it green with benchmarkrun-local result normalization.
- The current fix is in internal/benchmarkrun/results.go, intentionally leaving benchrunner raw sampling untouched and stripping only the successful-stop control-plane cancellation artifact.
- Next: run the documented Docker Compose reproduction to verify `/benchmark/results` no longer shows `stats.latest_error: "context canceled"`, then continue to full check/lint/test gates.

- Manual Docker Compose verification passed: after start/alter/stop on the documented quick-start stack, GET /benchmark/results returned status `stopped` with `stats.latest_error` empty.
- No second RED test was needed because the live path matched the intended fix on the first verification pass.
- Next: bring the Compose stack down, then run the required `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` gates before updating the task metadata.

- Required gates passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all succeeded after the benchmarkrun results normalization change.
- The fix remains boundary-local: stopped successful results now hide only the control-plane `context canceled` artifact while preserving the existing result shape and leaving benchrunner raw sampling untouched.
- Next: update the task file acceptance checklist and passes flag, run task_switch, then commit and push all changes.
